title: Philadelphia · Forty Years of Crime
subtitle: FBI UCR 1985–2005 + PPD offenses 2006–2026 · 21 districts, neighborhood names · real locations

copy.chapter2Kicker: CHAPTER 2 · THE LAST 5 YEARS · PPD / OPENDATAPHILLY
copy.chapter2Caption: Monthly reports, 2021–2026. Every dot is a real reported incident location (block-level, sampled).
copy.transitionDesc: The chart counted per year. From here the map counts per month across 21 police districts — every dot a real reported location, anonymized to the block.
copy.methodRecentSub: the last five years · month by month · real locations
copy.methodDotsSub: Every dot is a real reported incident location (block-level). 94.3% map to a current district — retired districts counted, disclosed, never redistributed.
copy.methodFootnote: FBI UCR (through 2005) and PPD incident data (2006+) are different measures — shown as two chapters, not one line.
copy.quizQuestion: Which Philadelphia district is the safest?
copy.seamExplain: The ruler changes here — not the city. Through 2005 the gray bars are the FBI's yearly tally of Philadelphia's classic “index” crimes — murder, robbery, assault, burglary, theft. From 2006 the figures are every offense PPD records — a far broader net than the FBI's eight index crimes — so the level steps up because the measure widened, not because crime jumped. Compare the shape within one color, never across the dashed line.
copy.sourceLine: Data: Philadelphia Police Department via OpenDataPhilly · block-level locations · provided as-is
copy.coverageText: 94.3% mapped to a current district · retired districts disclosed
copy.creditsSources: Sources: PPD incident data (2006–present) via OpenDataPhilly (City of Philadelphia license, as-is) & FBI UCR (1985–2005) via the FBI Crime Data Explorer. District boundaries: City of Philadelphia; district neighborhood labels derived from real incident locations vs the City's neighborhood layer. Basemap: © OpenStreetMap contributors (ODbL).

historyNotes.text: 1989: the peak — 116,314 crimes in one year.
2005: still above 82,000 a year as the FBI era ends.
The modern record: down by nearly a third since 2006.

annotations.text: Every dot is a real reported incident location — PPD via OpenDataPhilly.
August 2023 — the busiest month of the last five years: 15,131 mapped reports.
The 15th District — Frankford & Mayfair — Philadelphia's busiest for Group A crime.
The 18th — Cobbs Creek: down 13% since 2022, the city's biggest fall.
Citywide, recorded offenses fell 32% from 2006 to 2025 (223,356 → 152,563).
